problem_addressed: Creating embodied agents that can continuously explore, acquire diverse skills, and make novel discoveries without human intervention in open-ended environments.
core_claim: VOYAGER is the first LLM-powered embodied lifelong learning agent that achieves open-ended exploration and skill acquisition in Minecraft through its three key components, outperforming prior methods.
key_contributions: An automatic curriculum that maximizes exploration
An ever-growing skill library of executable code for storing and retrieving complex behaviors
A new iterative prompting mechanism that incorporates environment feedback, execution errors, and self-verification for program improvement
novelty: Unlike prior works that either use hierarchical reinforcement learning or LLMs as high-level planners with limited exploration flexibility, VOYAGER employs an automatic curriculum that unfolds in a bottom-up manner driven by curiosity, enabling truly open-ended exploration.
method_summary: VOYAGER interacts with GPT-4 via blackbox queries to generate executable code for actions in Minecraft, using Mineflayer as the low-level controller. The agent's three components work together to drive exploration, store skills, and improve programs iteratively.
method_components: Skill library for increasingly complex behaviors
Iterative prompting mechanism that uses code as action space
key_innovation: The integration of environment feedback, execution errors, and self-verification into an iterative prompting mechanism, with self-verification being the most critical component for deciding when to move on to new tasks or reattempt unsuccessful ones.
assumptions: GPT-4 provides sufficient code generation quality for the task
Minecraft's environment can be effectively represented through text-based feedback
The skill library approach can effectively store and retrieve complex behaviors
The automatic curriculum can effectively drive exploration
theoretical_grounding: The paper relies on empirical evaluation and ablation studies rather than formal theoretical proofs, demonstrating that VOYAGER obtains 3.3× more unique items, travels 2.3× longer distances, and unlocks key tech tree milestones up to 15.3× faster than prior SOTA.
title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
